event: Scenes from the life of Saint Barbara
symbols: tower — Saint Barbara's imprisonment by her father, her most recognizable attribute
scenes of violence — the martyrdom and persecution of the virgin saint
architectural settings — the various locations of Barbara's life story
roundels above — additional narrative episodes completing the hagiographic cycle
description: A wide fresco composition depicting multiple narrative scenes from the life of Saint Barbara. The composition includes architectural settings with towers and buildings, groups of figures in various actions — some appear to be scenes of confrontation or judgment, others of escape or pursuit. Circular roundels in the upper portion contain additional scenes. The fresco is somewhat worn and the small scale makes individual details difficult to discern, but the overall narrative structure of a vita cycle is clear.
